node.statement: N1.1 (BASE, pure algebra). Writing S_F := 2F - I_A in algebra A and S_Phi := 2Phi - I_B in algebra B, the intertwiner relation lifts to the shifted elements: S_F j = j S_Phi (as bounded linear maps B -> A). DERIVATION: S_F j = (2F - I_A) j = 2 (F j) - I_A j. By GT-hyp (H5) F j = j Phi; and I_A j = j (the identity on the A-side composed with j is j itself), while j I_B = j and by GT-hyp (H4) j(I_B)=I_A so j applied with the B-unit is consistent; precisely I_A j = j = j I_B as maps B->A. Hence S_F j = 2 (j Phi) - j = j (2Phi) - j = j (2Phi - I_B) = j S_Phi, using linearity of j (GT-hyp H3: j linear) to pull the scalar 2 and the unit I_B inside, and j I_B = j. Therefore S_F j = j S_Phi. Uses GT-hyp.
